label: Dyn. 0
note: Dyn 0 per Hendrickx Ch 2 p.88: 'Dyn. 0 has however been used with different meanings and the only consistency is the inclusion of Iry-Hor and Ka. Perhaps its use can be defended for referring to the line of kings from Abydos identified through inscriptions and tombs at the cemeteries U and B.' parent_period is null because Hendrickx does not assign Dyn 0 to the Early Dynastic Period — it sits at the Predynastic / Early Dynastic boundary (Naqada IIIA1 - IIIB per Table II.1.7). Absolute BCE dates left null: Hendrickx Table II.1.7 does not give bounded ranges for IIIA1 or IIIB.

Dyn 0. Hendrickx Ch 2 places Iry-Hor in Naqada IIIB (Table II.1.7, p.92) and identifies his tomb as B1/2 at Abydos cemetery B (Table II.1.6, p.89). Per p.88: Dyn 0 usage varies but the inclusion of Iry-Hor and Ka is the one consistency. Occasional spelling 'Irj-Hor' also at p.89. Absolute BCE range left null: Hendrickx Table II.1.7 gives bounded ranges for Naqada IIIA2 (ca. 3350-3150) and IIIC1 (ca. 3150-3100) but IIIB falls within that transition without an explicit range.

Dyn 0. Hendrickx reign-spans Ka across the late IIIB / start IIIC1 transition: Table II.1.7 (p.92) positions him at the end of Naqada IIIB (U-t, Iry-Hor – Ka); footnote 101 on p.84 states 'Stufe IIIc1 = Ka—Narmer'. Tomb is B7/9 at Abydos cemetery B (Table II.1.6 p.89). Per Hendrickx p.88 Ka is one of the two rulers (with Iry-Hor) whose Dyn-0 placement is universally accepted.

Cemetery U ruler at Naqada IIIA1 (Hendrickx Ch 2 p.91, Table II.1.7 p.92); tomb U-j is the famous pre-Narmer elite burial published by G. Dreyer (1998) *Umm el-Qaab I*. `dynasty: null` rather than 0: Hendrickx p.88 explicitly says 'the only consistency [in Dyn 0 usage] is the inclusion of Iry-Hor and Ka' — Scorpion I sits in cemetery U ahead of the cemetery-B Dyn-0 kings and Hendrickx does not consistently place him in Dyn 0. Phase-A consumers may elect Dyn 00 / Dyn 0 / null per local convention. Hendrickx p.91 notes that radiocarbon dates from U-j are problematic (appearing nearly contemporaneous with Hor-Aha, which is archaeologically excluded) — he suggests old-wood effects. Absolute BCE range left null: Hendrickx Table II.1.7 does not give an explicit range for Naqada IIIA1.

PDF prints '2119–2218' which is a typographical error; reconciled as 2119–2118 based on dynasty-end constraint (Dyn. 8 ends 2118) and all three transcriptions converging on this range. All three models read a '2' as first digit of end year; the '2' in '2218' is almost certainly a misprint of '1' giving '2118'.
